topic.what: Batch threshold encryption (BTE) scheme enabling committee-based selective decryption of ciphertext subsets while preserving privacy of non-selected items
topic.how: Uses punctured powers-of-tau public key with FFT-amenable middle-product computation for O(B log B) group exponentiations and O(B) pairings; ciphertexts are epochless and collision-free (no user-chosen index required)
topic.why_matters: Enables encrypted mempools for MEV mitigation in blockchain systems without epoch binding or index collision vulnerabilities that create censorship vectors

characteristics.what_it_measures: Total time to decrypt batch of B=512 ciphertexts on single core
characteristics.context: AWS Intel Xeon Platinum 8488C, single core, BLS12-381, AVX-512 vectorized implementation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Size of encrypted ciphertext including proof
characteristics.vs_baseline: Shortest among all BTE schemes; same as standard ElGamal
characteristics.evidence: Table 1 and text: ciphertext size = |G1| + |GT| vs 2|G1|+|GT| or 3|G1|+|GT| for other schemes
characteristics.context: Includes Schnorr-based NIZK proof for CCA security

characteristics.what_it_measures: Factor improvement over partial-fraction evaluation (PFE) baseline
characteristics.vs_baseline: 2.0× overall at B=512; 4.2× in open(B) phase
characteristics.evidence: Abstract and Table 4: B=512 total ~598ms vs ~1197ms for PFE
characteristics.context: Aggressively optimized shared codebase for fair comparison

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of bilinear pairings in precompute phase
characteristics.vs_baseline: B pairings for PFE but 4 FFTs vs 2 for BTX
characteristics.evidence: Table 2: precompute(B) = 2B pairings; 2 FFTs of size 2B

characteristics.what_itasures: Pairings per ciphertext in final opening phase
characteristics.evidence: Table 2 and Table 4: open(B) = B pairings; PFE requires 4B pairings
characteristics.context: BTX achieves 4.2× speedup in open(B) vs PFE

characteristics.what_it_measures: Communication per server in threshold decryption
characteristics.evidence: Section 5.2: each server sends single G1 element σj; communication is O(1) independent of B
characteristics.context: Threshold scheme with N servers, t corruption threshold

novelty: Collision-free epochless BTE without user-chosen batch index
FFT-amenable scheme achieving O(B log B) decryption complexity
Shortest ciphertext size matching standard ElGamal (|G1|+|GT|)
Single-round threshold protocol with O(1) per-server communication
open_problems: Secret key size grows linearly with batch size (sk = τ and Shamir shares of τ^i for i∈[Bmax])
CRS requires 2Bmax elements in G2 (all powers except τ^{Bmax+1})
Middle product FFT in GT has 3-5× higher concrete cost than source groups
Performance advantage over BEAT++ is implementation-dependent rather than structural
